what_it_does: Defines two React components for the Vi Workspace centre pane: TaskNode (a single labelled task node with a status dot, name, and optional meta) and TaskTree (renders an array of layers, each with a tag/description header and a grid of TaskNodes, with a visual connector between consecutive layers). Exposes TaskTree on window.
observations: TaskNode applies a CSS class from a `state` prop (e.g. idle/active/done/blocked drive styling via vi.css).
TaskTree maps over `layers`; each layer renders a 'Layer {i+1}' tag, the layer's `desc`, and its `nodes`.
A vi-layer-connector div is inserted between layers (only when i < layers.length - 1).
Uses React.Fragment with index keys for the layer+connector pair, and index keys for nodes.
Assigns to window.TaskTree (global) rather than using module exports.
Relies on a global React (no import statement).
standouts: The component is a pure projection of a `layers` data structure — layer count, node count, and states all come from props, so the three-layer architecture is data-driven not hardcoded.
conventions: Browser-global component pattern (window.TaskTree, global React, no imports/exports) — consistent with the other Vi kit files in this batch.
Class-name-from-state styling, delegating all visuals to vi.css.
concerns: Uses array index as React key for both layers and nodes — acceptable for a static demo list but fragile if lists reorder.
notes: Stateless, data-driven renderer for Vi's three-layer agent task tree; feed it a `layers` array of {desc, nodes:[{name, meta, state}]}.
questions: Which exact `state` values are valid? (idle/active/done/blocked appear in vi.css and App.jsx, but this file doesn't enumerate them.)
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: file comment says 'Vi's three-layer agent visualisation' and it renders an arbitrary-depth layered node tree driven by data.
apparent_themes: Data-driven UI (visual derived from a layers/nodes structure)
Making agent execution legible as a layered node tree
Separation of structure (JSX) from appearance (vi.css classes)
intention_signals: Tries to render Vi's live agent execution as a generic, reusable layered tree so any task decomposition can be visualised by passing the right data.
contribution: Contributes the concrete render component that turns Vi's three-layer agent model into a live, status-coloured layered node visualisation.
summary_for_embedding: React TaskTree/TaskNode components rendering Vi's three-layer agent architecture as a data-driven, status-coloured layered node tree, exposed on window.
intention_for_embedding: To visualise an AI agent's layered task decomposition (plan/spawn/execute) as a live status tree from a props-supplied layers structure.
